name: uxl-skill-evaluator
purpose: Measure whether UXL agent skills improve agent behavior versus a baseline agent without the skill.

arms.description: Agent receives the task and repository context but not the skill.
Agent is explicitly instructed to use the relevant UXL skill.
Agent receives normal repository/tool context and must decide whether to use the skill.
Agent receives the skill plus approved official documentation context.

tracks.description: Validate skill structure, source ledgers, links, wrappers, and eval definitions.
Compare baseline and skill-assisted answers on UXL-specific prompts.
Score tool use, skill invocation, validation commands, command count, and trace hygiene.
Run generated code or patches in isolated workspaces and verify outcomes.
Run selected executable tasks on trusted CPU, GPU, accelerator, or distributed backend runners.

dimensions.description: Chooses the right UXL API, dispatch model, execution policy, primitive, or collective workflow.
Provides build, link, environment, and runtime guidance that can configure and run.
Uses current official UXL/project sources for release, backend, hardware, and compatibility claims.
Requires reference outputs, tolerances, deterministic seeds, synchronization, and regression checks.
Separates correctness from performance and demands representative benchmarks before speedup claims.
Avoids false claims about support, compatibility, performance, or backend availability.
Gives concrete next steps, commands, checks, and artifacts.
Tracks token count, command count, tool churn, retries, and wall time.

hardware_tiers.description: GitHub-hosted CI for static checks and non-hardware evaluator validation.
CPU runner with the compiler/runtime and UXL library dependencies needed for CPU tasks.
SYCL-capable GPU runner with a supported backend runtime for GPU tasks.
Multi-rank or multi-node runner for oneCCL and distributed oneDAL tasks.
